rationale: Both papers center on repeated choice/conjoint elicitation and out-of-sample predictive validity: the citing paper studies how additional questions can reduce external validity, while the cited paper models information-poor respondents (“gremlins”) and their impact on hit rates and pricing predictions. That makes the cited work a plausible method/mechanism source or competing explanation for validity loss in conjoint settings, not an obvious incidental mention. With only DOI-level match evidence but strong conceptual overlap, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
